headlines.headline: Israel Strikes Historic Port of Tyre in Lebanon, Killing Eight
headlines.summary: Israeli forces struck the southern Lebanese city of Tyre, killing at least eight people in an escalation that adds strain to peace efforts in the broader Middle East conflict.

headlines.headline: Germany Scraps Franco-German Fighter Jet Program in Blow to European Defense
headlines.summary: Germany has pulled out of a joint fighter jet program with France, dealing a significant setback to European defense cooperation and raising questions about the continent's military independence.

headlines.headline: US Trade Deficit Narrows as Exports Hit Record High in April
headlines.summary: The US trade deficit shrank in April as exports surged to unprecedented levels, bolstered by energy shipments and potentially contributing to quarterly economic growth.

headlines.headline: China Exports Surge Past Forecasts on Global AI Boom Demand
headlines.summary: China's export growth accelerated in May, driven by strong demand for chips, electric vehicles, and high-tech goods fueling the global AI boom, providing relief amid energy price pressures.

headlines.headline: US Lawmakers Push Tighter Rules on Chipmakers Supplying Chinese Firms
headlines.summary: A bipartisan pair of senators urged the Trump administration to tighten restrictions on contract chipmakers like TSMC to prevent advanced AI chips from reaching overseas subsidiaries of Chinese companies.

headlines.headline: UN Chief Guterres "deeply alarmed" by Middle East violence escalation
headlines.summary: UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres called for an end to violence in the Middle East, expressing deep alarm at renewed escalation between Israel and Iran as diplomatic efforts continue.

headlines.headline: Drones from Ukraine war straying into NATO territory, raising tensions
headlines.summary: Russian and Ukrainian drones are veering off course and entering airspace of NATO countries like Lithuania and Romania, creating security concerns for nations not directly involved in the conflict.

headlines.headline: Congo Ebola outbreak reaches 100+ deaths amid conflict zone challenges
headlines.summary: The Ebola outbreak in eastern Congo has killed over 100 people out of 550 cases in less than a month, with medical response hampered by ongoing conflict in the region.

perspectives.angle: Congo Ebola outbreak reaches 100 deaths from 550 cases as conflict hampers response
perspectives.summary: At least 100 people have died from Ebola in eastern Congo since the outbreak was declared on May 15, with 550 confirmed cases. Armed conflict, attacks on health workers, and lack of approved vaccine or treatment are complicating containment efforts.

headlines.headline: US Apache helicopter crashes near Strait of Hormuz, crew rescued
headlines.summary: A US Army AH-64 Apache attack helicopter went down near the strategic Strait of Hormuz for unknown reasons. Both crew members were safely rescued, according to US officials.

headlines.headline: China shares first far-side moon samples with Russian scientists
headlines.summary: Beijing delivered lunar samples collected during the Chang'e-6 mission to Russian researchers, marking the first time foreign scientists have access to far-side moon material.

headlines.headline: Russia conducts naval drills in Baltic Sea during NATO exercises
headlines.summary: Russia's navy held military drills in the Baltic Sea practicing missile launches and bombing runs while NATO conducted major exercises nearby, highlighting ongoing tensions.

headlines.headline: Satellite images suggest Israeli air base hit in Iranian missile strikes
headlines.summary: Analysis of satellite imagery indicates possible damage to a hangar at Ramat David Air Force Base following Iran's recent missile attacks, despite Israeli claims of minimal impact.

headlines.headline: US troops test German Helsing AI attack drones in Europe
headlines.summary: German defense company Helsing armed US soldiers with HX-2 drones for exercises in Lithuania, where they successfully spotted and engaged targets in joint drills.

headlines.headline: Car bombing kills man in Russian military housing district near Moscow
headlines.summary: A vehicle explosion in a residential area for military personnel outside Moscow killed one person, with Ukrainian media reporting it targeted a Russian officer.

headlines.headline: UK Sanctions Networks Financing West Bank Settler Violence
headlines.summary: Britain announced coordinated sanctions with five international partners targeting networks involved in financing, enabling and carrying out settler violence in the occupied West Bank.

headlines.headline: France Bans Israeli Finance Minister Smotrich Over Gaza Recolonisation Call
headlines.summary: France barred entry to far-right Finance Minister Bezalel Smotrich, condemning his calls for Palestinian resettlement and recolonisation of Gaza, as part of coordinated sanctions with five allied nations.

headlines.headline: Bulgaria Halts All Military Aid to Ukraine
headlines.summary: Bulgaria Defense Minister Dimitar Stoyanov announced the country will no longer send weapons to Ukraine, marking a significant shift in European support for Kyiv.

headlines.headline: Finland Moves to Allow Nuclear Weapons Transport in Crisis Situations
headlines.summary: Finland defense committee backed legal changes to permit nuclear weapons transport during crises, moving restrictions into criminal code while allowing allied nuclear arms transit.

perspectives.angle: Finland Considers Allowing Nuclear Weapons Import in Crises
perspectives.summary: Finlands parliamentary defence committee backs legislation to allow nuclear weapons imports during crisis situations, marking a significant shift for the traditionally non-nuclear Nordic nation.

headlines.headline: China Exports Surge Past Forecasts on AI-Driven Demand
headlines.summary: Chinese export growth accelerated in May, beating expectations on strong demand for chips, autos and high-tech goods fueling the global AI boom.

perspectives.angle: China Exports Surge on Chip Demand and Front-Loaded Orders
perspectives.summary: China's export growth likely strengthened in May as companies rushed orders ahead of energy price pressures from the Gulf conflict, while global semiconductor demand remained robust, a Reuters poll showed.

headlines.headline: Philippines protests China's floating structure on disputed South China Sea shoal
headlines.summary: Manila has protested Beijing's deployment of a floating structure with personnel on Scarborough Shoal, a contested feature in the South China Sea. The move escalates tensions in the strategically vital waterway.

headlines.headline: Xi cements sway over North Korea as Kim names China top priority
headlines.summary: Kim Jong Un feted Chinese President Xi Jinping with lavish celebrations in Pyongyang, calling ties with China a strategic priority. The visit underscores deepening alignment between Beijing and Pyongyang amid regional tensions.

headlines.headline: Trump approval near record low as Americans expect higher gas prices
headlines.summary: Only 22% of Americans approve of how President Trump is managing the cost of living, with most expecting higher gasoline prices amid Middle East tensions affecting global oil markets.

headlines.headline: Investors see tipping point for bonds after Iran conflict shreds old playbooks
headlines.summary: Bonds failed to shield investors during the Iran-Israel conflict, but fund managers say debt may reclaim its safe-haven role if inflation starts hitting growth, marking a potential shift in market dynamics.

perspectives.angle: Investors See Tipping Point for Bonds After Geopolitics Shreds Old Playbooks
perspectives.summary: Bonds have failed to shield investors during the Iran-Israel conflict, but some fund managers believe debt markets may soon reclaim their safe-haven role if inflation begins to impact economic growth.

headlines.headline: Iran Confirms Two Military Personnel Killed in Israeli Strikes
headlines.summary: Iranian state media reports two high-ranking air defense personnel killed in Israeli strikes on Monday, as diplomatic sources caution that a deal to end the US-Iran conflict within days is highly unlikely.

headlines.headline: Israeli Ministers Call for Bombing Beirut Over Hezbollah
headlines.summary: Senior Israeli ministers call for striking the Lebanese capital and detaining women in retaliation against Hezbollah, escalating rhetoric amid ongoing cross-border conflict.

headlines.headline: Secret Camera Found in UK Office Where China Embassy Approved
headlines.summary: A hidden camera was discovered in the Whitehall building where UK ministers approved a new Chinese embassy, raising security concerns at a sensitive time for UK-China relations.

headlines.headline: EU to Discuss Deporting Afghan Refugees with Taliban
headlines.summary: European countries are preparing to discuss deportation agreements with the Taliban government for Afghan refugees, a controversial move as the bloc faces migration pressures.

headlines.headline: US blacklists Alibaba, Baidu, BYD as military-linked Chinese companies
headlines.summary: The Pentagon added dozens of Chinese companies including tech giant Alibaba, EV maker BYD, and search engine Baidu to its list of firms allegedly aiding Beijing's military, a move Beijing condemned as discriminatory.

perspectives.angle: Pentagon adds Alibaba, BYD, Baidu to Chinese military companies list
perspectives.summary: The US Defense Department added major Chinese tech firms including Alibaba, Baidu, and BYD to a list of companies believed to aid China's military, escalating tensions with Beijing and warning American businesses about potential risks.

headlines.headline: Xi Jinping wraps up rare North Korea visit with deeper cooperation pledge
headlines.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping completed his first visit to North Korea in seven years, establishing a deeper understanding and clearer path for development of ties between the two countries.

perspectives.angle: Xi Jinping Visits North Korea for First Summit in Seven Years
perspectives.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping arrived in Pyongyang for his first visit in seven years, meeting with Kim Jong Un. The summit follows Putin's recent visit and signals deepening coordination between China and North Korea.

perspectives.angle: China's Xi vows unwavering support for North Korea in rare Pyongyang visit
perspectives.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping made a rare visit to North Korea, pledging to safeguard common interests and fight 'hegemony' alongside Kim Jong Un, signaling deepening ties amid regional tensions.

headlines.headline: IAEA urges Iran to re-engage as West pushes nuclear resolution
headlines.summary: UN nuclear watchdog chief Rafael Grossi called on Iran to resume inspections at sites bombed by the US and Israel, as Washington led efforts for a resolution at the agency's board meeting.

perspectives.angle: IAEA Urges Iran to Re-Engage on Nuclear Inspections
perspectives.summary: UN nuclear watchdog chief Rafael Grossi called on Iran to allow inspections at sites bombed by the US and Israel. The US led a push for a resolution at the IAEA board demanding Iran resume cooperation.

perspectives.angle: US pushes IAEA resolution demanding Iran account for bombed nuclear sites
perspectives.summary: The US is lobbying countries on the IAEA Board to back a resolution requiring Iran to disclose what happened to its bombed nuclear facilities and enriched uranium stocks.

headlines.headline: Iran World Cup ticket allocation revoked days before tournament
headlines.summary: Iran's football federation said FIFA revoked its ticket allocation for fans at World Cup games in the US, leaving supporters with travel plans stranded.

perspectives.angle: Iran Says US Revoked World Cup Ticket Allocation for Supporters
perspectives.summary: Iran's football federation says the US has withdrawn its World Cup ticket allocation, leaving the nation unable to provide tickets to supporters for the tournament.

headlines.headline: Maine Senate primary under scrutiny over candidate's past
headlines.summary: Democratic Senate candidate Graham Platner faces voter scrutiny over online posts, a Nazi-linked tattoo, and explicit text allegations in a race that could determine party control of the US Senate.

perspectives.angle: Maine Senate Candidate Platner Faces Intimidation Allegations
perspectives.summary: Democratic Senate candidate Graham Platner denies reports of physical intimidation toward women as scandals shadow the Maine Democratic primary race.

headlines.headline: China prepares $295 billion plan for nationwide AI infrastructure buildout
headlines.summary: China is preparing to spend around 2 trillion yuan over five years on data centers across the country to challenge the US in the AI race, as geopolitical tensions drive technology competition.

headlines.headline: Zelenskyy arrives in Estonia for Nordic-Baltic summit on Ukraine support
headlines.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy is in Estonia to attend a summit of Nordic and Baltic leaders, seeking continued military and financial support as Ukraine presses its counteroffensive against Russia.

headlines.headline: German exports rise unexpectedly in April despite Iran war headwinds
headlines.summary: German exports rose by 0.9% in April compared to the previous month, defying analyst expectations of a decline amid tensions in the Middle East and global supply chain disruptions.

perspectives.angle: Chinas Exports Surge 19.4% Year-on-Year Despite War Conditions
perspectives.summary: Chinas exports posted a 19.4% year-on-year increase in May, defying expectations amid severe effects from ongoing conflict in the Middle East and global supply chain disruptions.

headlines.headline: Trump confirms US Army Apache helicopter crew safe after incident near Strait of Hormuz
headlines.summary: President Trump said the pilots of a US Army Apache helicopter that went down near the strategic Strait of Hormuz are safe, amid ongoing tensions between Iran and Israel.

headlines.headline: Microsoft Cuts Hundreds of Cloud Jobs in China as Data Laws Tighten
headlines.summary: Microsoft is laying off employees at its China cloud unit amid ongoing restrictions on cross-border data flows between Beijing and Washington, reflecting growing tech tensions.

headlines.headline: China Firmly Opposes US Blacklisting of Alibaba, Baidu and Other Firms
headlines.summary: Beijing condemned Washington's decision to blacklist major Chinese companies including Alibaba and Baidu, vowing to defend its interests.

headlines.headline: Pakistan and Lebanon Army Chiefs Meet as Regional Mediation Continues
headlines.summary: The heads of the Pakistani and Lebanese armed forces agreed to boost cooperation during talks in Pakistan as Middle East diplomatic efforts drag on.

headlines.headline: Global Conflicts Reach Highest Level Since World War II
headlines.summary: A new report shows global conflicts surged in 2025, reaching levels not seen since World War II, as simultaneous wars in Ukraine, Middle East, and other regions strain international institutions.

headlines.headline: Saudi Contractor Scraps $800 Million IPO Citing Iran War Uncertainty
headlines.summary: Saudi Arabia's Mutlaq Al-Ghowairi Contracting Company cancelled its planned IPO in Riyadh that could have raised up to $800 million, marking one of the first regional offerings shelved since the start of the Iran war.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Recaptures Over 600 Square Kilometers in 2026
headlines.summary: Ukraine's military chief says Kyiv has retaken more than 600 square kilometers of territory in 2026, with independent battlefield mappers confirming Russia's advances have slowed or reversed in recent months.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine recaptures over 600 square kilometers of territory in 2026
perspectives.summary: Ukraine's military chief reports retaking more than 600 sq km this year, with independent battlefield mappers confirming Russia's advances have slowed or reversed in recent months.

headlines.headline: Taiwan simulates destroying Chinese invasion force in coastal drill
headlines.summary: Taiwan's military conducted a live-fire exercise firing rockets and artillery to repel an amphibious assault, describing it as a more realistic combat scenario with reduced preparation time amid heightened tensions with Beijing.

headlines.headline: Kenyan police fire tear gas at protesters opposing US Ebola quarantine center
headlines.summary: Police in Nanyuki scattered demonstrators opposing a US-built Ebola quarantine facility for Americans, which Kenyan courts had ordered to halt. The facility was rapidly constructed amid Congo's growing outbreak.

headlines.headline: France and Germany scrap $100 billion joint fighter jet program
headlines.summary: The leaders of Germany and France have agreed to abandon the FCAS sixth-generation fighter jet project after years of industrial disputes over Europe's most ambitious defense program, marking a significant setback for European military cooperation.

headlines.headline: US leads record global surge in nuclear weapons spending
headlines.summary: The United States spent $69.2 billion on nuclear arsenals in 2025, more than all other nuclear states combined and representing a 22% increase, according to a campaign group report. Global nuclear spending hit a record high as major powers modernize their deterrents.

perspectives.angle: Nuclear Weapons Spending Hits Record $119 Billion as Arms Race Intensifies
perspectives.summary: Global spending on nuclear weapons rose 19% in 2025 to $119 billion, the highest since tracking began. The US led with $69.2 billion, more than all other nuclear states combined, while China increased spending by 7% to $13.5 billion.

headlines.headline: France and Germany Halt €100B FCAS Fighter Jet Program
headlines.summary: Long-running disputes over technology sharing, industrial workshare and intellectual property rights have forced France and Germany to scrap their landmark next-generation fighter jet project, dealing a major blow to European defense ambitions.

perspectives.angle: France and Germany cancel €100 billion FCAS joint fighter jet program
perspectives.summary: Europe's most ambitious collaborative defense program has been scrapped after France and Germany failed to resolve disputes over intellectual property and work sharing between Airbus and Dassault Aviation. The cancellation comes as Western military leaders warn of growing threats from Russia.

headlines.headline: Israel Orders Evacuation of Lebanons Tyre Including Christian Quarter
headlines.summary: The Israeli military issued an evacuation order for the Lebanese city of Tyre, including its Christian quarter, as hostilities with Hezbollah continue amid broader regional tensions.

headlines.headline: Russian Drone and Missile Strikes Kill 3 Injure 18 in Kharkiv Oblast
headlines.summary: Russian forces launched 11 drone strikes on Kharkiv, with additional missile attacks killing 3 and injuring 18 as Ukraine continues to face sustained aerial bombardment.

headlines.headline: Philippines earthquake death toll rises as rescuers search rubble
headlines.summary: A powerful 7.8-magnitude earthquake struck the southern Philippines, killing at least 37 people and injuring hundreds. Rescuers continued searching collapsed buildings for survivors amid ongoing aftershocks.

perspectives.angle: Philippines earthquake displaces 32,000 as death toll rises to 37
perspectives.summary: The 7.8 magnitude earthquake that struck the Philippines on Monday has displaced over 32,000 people and killed at least 37, making it one of the strongest quakes to hit the country in half a century.

headlines.headline: US judge strikes down Trump's $100,000 H-1B visa fee as unlawful
headlines.summary: A federal judge ruled that the Trump administration's $100,000 fee on new H-1B visas for skilled foreign workers was an unlawful tax not authorized by Congress, blocking a policy that tech companies had opposed.

perspectives.angle: US Judge Rules Trump H-1B Visa Fee Unlawful
perspectives.summary: A federal judge declared the $100,000 fee imposed by President Trump on new H-1B visas for highly skilled workers unlawful, dealing a blow to the administration immigration restrictions.

headlines.headline: Xi Jinping visits North Korea for first time since 2019
headlines.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping met Kim Jong Un in Pyongyang in a summit aimed at strengthening bilateral ties, as North Korea has grown closer to Russia amid the Ukraine war.

headlines.headline: Somali World Cup referee denied entry to US days before tournament
headlines.summary: FIFA referee Omar Abdulkadir Artan was denied entry to the United States just days before the 2026 World Cup, ending his historic opportunity to become the first Somali to officiate at the tournament.

headlines.headline: US Army Apache Helicopter Crashes Near Strait of Hormuz, Crew Rescued
headlines.summary: A US Army Apache gunship went down near the strategic Strait of Hormuz waterway. President Trump confirmed both pilots are safe, though the cause remains unclear amid ongoing tensions with Iran in the region.

headlines.headline: Ebola Death Toll in DR Congo Climbs to 101
headlines.summary: The Democratic Republic of Congo is fighting its 17th Ebola outbreak since the virus was first identified in 1976, with the death toll now exceeding 100 as health authorities struggle to contain the spread.

headlines.headline: UN Urges Afghan Taliban to End Arrests of Women Over Dress Rules
headlines.summary: The United Nations mission in Afghanistan expressed concern over the detention of women in a western province for allegedly failing to comply with dress requirements, urging Taliban authorities to treat all people equally.

headlines.headline: WHO Chief Urges Uganda to Reconsider Congo Border Closure Over Ebola
headlines.summary: WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus said Uganda should reconsider its decision to close its border with the Democratic Republic of Congo due to the ongoing Ebola outbreak.

perspectives.angle: WHO urges Uganda to reconsider Congo border closure over Ebola outbreak
perspectives.summary: The WHO chief called on Uganda to reconsider closing its border with DR Congo, where confirmed Ebola cases have risen to 515 with over 100 deaths. Armed groups threaten response efforts.

headlines.headline: UN report warns of deepening crisis in world's oceans
headlines.summary: A major UN assessment by 600 scientists found oceans warming and rising faster than ever, with 16% of ocean heat increase since 1955 occurring since 2018. Sea level rise has more than doubled to 4.3mm per year, as marine ecosystems approach critical tipping points.

headlines.headline: Indonesia reaffirms Myanmar as ASEAN family in visit to junta leader
headlines.summary: Indonesia's Foreign Minister Sugiono met Myanmar's junta chief-turned-president Min Aung Hlaing, delivering President Prabowo's message that Myanmar remains part of the ASEAN family. The visit comes two months after the general who ousted an elected government was sworn in as president.

headlines.headline: UAE deepens alignment with US bloc despite conciliatory rhetoric on Iran
headlines.summary: Analysis shows the UAE is distinguishing itself as the GCC's most hawkish member on Iran, even as other Gulf states pursue diplomatic engagement with Tehran. The Emirates are deepening their alignment with the American bloc despite recent conciliatory comments.

headlines.headline: ICC prosecutor Karim Khan suspended amid sexual misconduct investigation
headlines.summary: The International Criminal Courts chief prosecutor has been suspended while member states consider disciplinary measures, marking a major crisis for the tribunal investigating war crimes in Ukraine and Gaza.

perspectives.angle: ICC chief prosecutor Karim Khan suspended over sexual misconduct allegations
perspectives.summary: In an unprecedented move, the embattled chief prosecutor of the International Criminal Court was suspended from his duties pending a decision by an oversight body on sexual misconduct allegations.

headlines.headline: Iran and Israel halt military operations after 48-hour exchange of fire
headlines.summary: Both countries called a halt to military operations after the most intense exchange of fire in months. Irans president said the country had not abandoned peace negotiations despite the escalation.

perspectives.angle: Iran and Israel Pull Back After Fierce Exchange of Attacks
perspectives.summary: The conflict between Israel and Hezbollah, an Iranian-backed militia, is complicating efforts to end the war in Iran as both sides stepped back from further escalation following intense strikes.

headlines.headline: Kremlin admits fuel supply problems after Ukrainian strikes on Russian energy sites
headlines.summary: Kremlin spokesperson Dmitry Peskov acknowledged certain problems with fuel supplies in southern Russia after a surge in Ukrainian airstrikes on Russian energy and fuel infrastructure disrupted distribution.

headlines.headline: Israel and Iran Halt Strikes After Trump Warning
headlines.summary: Israel and Iran paused military strikes following a direct appeal from US President Donald Trump, who warned Netanyahu that Israel would be "on your own" if it resumed attacks. The temporary ceasefire comes after months of conflict that has disrupted global oil markets.

perspectives.angle: Iran and Israel Halt Strikes After Trump Appeal
perspectives.summary: Iran and Israel said they halted attacks on each other after US President Donald Trump called for an immediate ceasefire, though Tehran warned it would resume strikes if Israel continued hitting Hezbollah in Lebanon.

perspectives.angle: Israel Defies Trump With Iran Strikes to Gain Peace Table Influence
perspectives.summary: Israel launched renewed strikes on Iran in apparent defiance of Donald Trump, seeking leverage in peace negotiations where it has been kept at arm's length by the US president.

headlines.headline: Oil Prices Edge Higher Amid Iran-Israel Uncertainty
headlines.summary: Oil prices rose slightly as markets awaited clarity on whether the Iran-Israel halt would hold. The Strait of Hormuz remains a focal point, with Iran's envoy saying transit fees would apply.

perspectives.angle: Oil prices surge more than $4 as Israel-Iran strikes threaten ceasefire
perspectives.summary: Oil prices jumped by more than $4 per barrel as Israel conducted strikes on Iran and Lebanon, threatening the fragile ceasefire. Brent crude neared $98 despite OPEC+ approving another output hike.

headlines.headline: Lebanon Says Israel Bombed It Nearly 3,500 Times During Ceasefire
headlines.summary: Israel conducted nearly 3,500 airstrikes on Lebanon since the April ceasefire announcement, Lebanon's defense minister said. Lebanese President Aoun appealed directly to Israel to pursue negotiations instead of war.

perspectives.angle: Lebanon reports nearly 3,500 Israeli strikes during ceasefire period
perspectives.summary: Lebanon's defense minister says Israel carried out nearly 3,500 airstrikes and hundreds of controlled explosions since the U.S.-announced ceasefire on April 16, highlighting continued violence despite formal truce.

headlines.headline: Malaysia opposition in turmoil as Islamist party PAS cuts ties with Bersatu
headlines.summary: PAS ended its cooperation with Bersatu, gutting the Perikatan Nasional opposition coalition ahead of key elections in Malaysia.

headlines.headline: Asian stocks track Wall Street tech bounce as oil eases on Mideast ceasefire hopes
headlines.summary: Asian markets rebounded as investors returned to AI stocks while crude prices eased following Israel-Iran hostilities pause.

headlines.headline: South Korea Names First Female Prime Minister in Decades to Lead AI Push
headlines.summary: President Lee Jae Myung nominated former Naver CEO Han Seong-sook as prime minister, tasking her with driving South Korea's AI-driven economic transformation.

perspectives.angle: South Korea Names First Female Prime Minister as President Lee Marks One Year
perspectives.summary: President Lee Jae-myung appointed Han Seong-sook as South Koreas first female PM in decades, as his administration focuses on AI development and pragmatic diplomacy with North Korea.

headlines.headline: EU Prepares 21st Sanctions Package Against Russia Worth $1.5 Trillion
headlines.summary: The European Union is finalizing its 21st sanctions package against Russia, targeting additional sectors to increase economic pressure on Moscow.

headlines.headline: Xi and Kim Agree to Expand Cooperation at Pyongyang Summit
headlines.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping and North Korean leader Kim Jong Un held a rare summit in Pyongyang, agreeing to expand cooperation in politics, economy and culture.

perspectives.angle: Kim Jong Un hosts Xi Jinping from position of rare strength in Pyongyang summit
perspectives.summary: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un gave Chinas Xi Jinping a grand welcome at a rare summit in Pyongyang, projecting unity while Xi sought to remind Kim that China remains the senior partner in their relationship.

headlines.headline: Iran Blames US for Latest Exchanges of Fire with Israel
headlines.summary: Iranian Foreign Ministry says overnight strikes will worsen an already chaotic diplomatic process with Washington, exchanging messages in an atmosphere of extreme suspicion.

perspectives.angle: Iran Blames US for Latest Exchanges of Fire with Israel
perspectives.summary: Iran warned that overnight exchanges of fire with Israel will worsen the already chaotic diplomatic process with the United States. Tehran said it was exchanging messages with Washington in an atmosphere of extreme suspicion.

headlines.headline: Poland Seeks Calm with Ukraine Over WWII Army Unit Dispute
headlines.summary: Polish PM Tusk calls for solidarity and talks after diplomatic relations deteriorated over Ukraine naming an army unit for nationalist insurgents who massacred Poles in World War Two.

perspectives.angle: Poland Seeks Dialogue with Ukraine Over Army Unit Row
perspectives.summary: Poland called for solidarity and talks with Ukraine after diplomatic relations deteriorated over the naming of a Ukrainian army unit for nationalist insurgents who massacred Poles during World War Two.

headlines.headline: Gulf Stock Markets Drop Amid Renewed Middle East Tensions
headlines.summary: Most stock markets in the Gulf ended lower on Monday as renewed conflict in the Middle East weighed on investor sentiment.

perspectives.angle: Gulf stock markets drop as Middle East tensions weigh on investor sentiment
perspectives.summary: Most Gulf stock markets ended lower on Monday as renewed conflict in the Middle East rattled investors despite ceasefire talks between Iran and Israel.

perspectives.angle: Gulf markets fall amid fresh US-Iran escalation
perspectives.summary: Most Gulf stock markets closed lower on Sunday after new escalations clouded efforts to end the war between the US and Iran, adding to regional economic uncertainty.

headlines.headline: US Military Disables Oil Tanker Headed to Iran in Gulf of Oman
headlines.summary: US forces disabled a Palau-flagged oil tanker in the Gulf of Oman as the unladen vessel headed toward Iran, escalating enforcement of the US naval blockade amid the ongoing Iran-Israel conflict.

headlines.headline: Iran Threatens to Defeat US Naval Blockade as Tensions Persist
headlines.summary: Iran top negotiator vowed to turn the US naval blockade into another defeat for the enemy, as Tehran faces increased maritime interdiction of its oil exports during the ongoing conflict with Israel.

headlines.headline: Russia's fuel crisis deepens as Ukraine steps up strikes on occupied territories
headlines.summary: Ukrainian attacks on Russian supply lines have made it difficult for Moscow to provide military and civilian supplies to occupied territories, intensifying pressure on Russian logistics.

headlines.headline: Flesh-Eating Screwworm Parasite Spreads Beyond Texas, Canada Blocks Livestock Imports
headlines.summary: The USDA confirmed three new screwworm cases including one outside Texas, in New Mexico. The flesh-eating parasite poses a threat to the cattle industry, with five confirmed cases so far including calves, a goat, and a dog.

headlines.headline: Zelensky Holds "Very Positive" Call With US Envoys Witkoff and Kushner
headlines.summary: Ukrainian President discussed Moscow's intentions and peace efforts with Trump's envoys in a positive phone call amid ongoing negotiations.

headlines.headline: Netanyahu Openly Defies Trump With Iran Strike Amid Lebanon Pressure
headlines.summary: Israeli PM's decision to strike Iran despite Trump's ceasefire demands highlights growing rift and Israel's attempt to secure influence in peace negotiations.

headlines.headline: Pentagon Policy Sparks Controversy Among Mormon Community
headlines.summary: Defense Department policy changes have drawn criticism from Mormon service members, prompting the military to adjust certain regulations.

headlines.headline: Bipartisan Insulin Legislation Gains Momentum in Congress
headlines.summary: Congressional efforts to cap insulin prices are advancing with bipartisan support, addressing long-standing healthcare cost concerns.

headlines.headline: India Inflation Rises to 4% on Food and Fuel Costs
headlines.summary: India's inflation likely rose to the Reserve Bank's 4% target in May, driven by higher vegetable prices and fuel costs linked to the US-Israel conflict with Iran, a Reuters poll indicated.

headlines.headline: China Begins Large-Scale Delivery of Gallium Chips for 6G Network
headlines.summary: Beijing is deploying millions of third-generation semiconductors using silicon-based technology for a planned space-ground 6G communications network, advancing its strategic tech capabilities amid export controls.

headlines.headline: Taiwan Hits Back at Beijing's "Cognitive Warfare" After Coastguard Patrols
headlines.summary: Taiwan's defense minister accused Beijing of "cognitive warfare" after Chinese coastguard vessels patrolled waters around the island, saying the operation aimed to assert jurisdiction over areas Beijing does not control.

headlines.headline: Russia Rejects Ukrainian, European Peace Initiatives, Says Battlefield Will Decide War
headlines.summary: Senior Russian officials effectively rejected recent Ukrainian and European proposals aimed at restarting peace talks, signaling that Moscow sees military gains as the path to resolution.

headlines.headline: Polish Concerns Over Ukraine EU Entry "Solved," Enlargement Chief Says
headlines.summary: The EU's enlargement chief Marta Kos said concerns raised by Poland over Ukraine's EU accession have been addressed at the working level, potentially smoothing the path for Kyiv's membership bid.

headlines.headline: NATO's Ankara Summit: A Reckoning for the Alliance
headlines.summary: As NATO allies confront uncertainties on multiple fronts, the upcoming Ankara Summit may become the alliance's most important meeting of the decade, with Turkey playing host amid shifting regional dynamics.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo XIV warns of 'profound crisis' in historic address to Spanish parliament
headlines.summary: In his first speech to a national parliament, Pope Leo XIV called for respect for migrants and international law, warning that escalating conflicts and polarization have pushed the world into a deep crisis.

perspectives.angle: Pope Leo XIV visits Barcelona, addresses Spain's cultural tensions
perspectives.summary: Pope Leo XIV navigated Spain's culture wars during his Barcelona visit, addressing Catalan language issues and calling for migrant rights in a historic parliamentary speech that received a 7-minute ovation.

headlines.headline: Magnitude 7.8 earthquake kills at least 35 in southern Philippines
headlines.summary: A powerful 7.8-magnitude earthquake struck off Mindanao island, killing at least 35 people and injuring dozens. Buildings collapsed in General Santos City as authorities launched search and rescue operations.

perspectives.angle: Philippines Earthquake Kills 19, Leaves 12 Missing
perspectives.summary: A powerful 7.8-magnitude earthquake struck off the southern coast of the Philippines, killing at least 19 people and leaving 12 missing as landslides damaged buildings.

perspectives.angle: Philippines Verifying at Least Five Deaths After 7.8 Magnitude Quake
perspectives.summary: Authorities are confirming casualties in General Santos city after a powerful 7.8 earthquake struck off Mindanao island, triggering tsunami warnings across the Pacific region.

headlines.headline: Apple unveils AI-powered Siri overhaul at WWDC 2026
headlines.summary: Apple announced a major revamp of its voice assistant at its annual developers conference, introducing 'Siri AI' with better voice recognition and a standalone app, marking the company's long-awaited push into generative AI.

headlines.headline: Taiwan calls Chinese Coast Guard patrols to its east provocative
headlines.summary: Taiwan defense minister condemned Chinese Coast Guard patrols east of the island as provocative, vowing military coordination with the coast guard to respond.

headlines.headline: Chinese military maneuvered near Taiwan as global tech executives gathered
headlines.summary: A hostile maritime exchange with China developed as Nvidia, Intel and other tech leaders attended a major Taiwan tech showcase, underscoring the island strategic importance.

perspectives.angle: Chinese warships shadow global executives at Taiwan AI tech summit
perspectives.summary: As Nvidia, Intel and other tech leaders gathered for Taiwan's Computex, Chinese military vessels conducted operations nearby, underscoring tensions over the island that hosts the world's most advanced chip production.

headlines.headline: South Korea to unveil major investment project as AI boom swells tax revenue
headlines.summary: President Lee Jae Myung announced plans for a large-scale investment project, citing buoyant tax revenues from the chip sector amid the AI boom.

headlines.headline: Bennett slams Netanyahu for "normalizing" Iran attacks
headlines.summary: Former Israeli PM Naftali Bennett criticized Netanyahu for accepting periodic Iranian strikes as routine, arguing Israel should not become accustomed to being attacked.

headlines.headline: Knesset passes law deducting terror victim compensation from PA taxes
headlines.summary: Israeli parliament approved legislation to deduct compensation for terror victims from Palestinian Authority tax revenues over "pay-for-slay" stipends paid to attackers families.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Jails Former Supreme Court Chief Justice for Graft
headlines.summary: The former head of Ukraine's Supreme Court was sentenced to five years in prison under a plea deal in a high-profile bribery case, as Kyiv's anti-corruption crackdown continues.

headlines.headline: Ukrainian drone strike kills one in Russian-annexed Crimea
headlines.summary: A Ukrainian drone struck a train in Crimea, killing its assistant driver and injuring the driver, according to Russian-installed governor Sergei Aksyonov. The attack is part of ongoing Ukrainian strikes on Russian logistics.

perspectives.angle: Ukrainian drone strike kills one in Russian-annexed Crimea
perspectives.summary: A Ukrainian drone struck a train in Crimea, killing its assistant driver and injuring the driver. The attack highlights ongoing Ukrainian strikes deep in Russian-occupied territories.

headlines.headline: South Korea KOSPI crashes over 8% as Fed fears spark tech rout
headlines.summary: South Korea stock benchmark plunged over 8%, tripping circuit breakers, after robust US jobs data lifted bets on a Federal Reserve rate hike and unleashed a selloff in tech-heavy markets that had powered the AI rally.

perspectives.angle: South Korea Halts Trading as KOSPI Plunges Over 8% in Tech-Led Selloff
perspectives.summary: South Korea activated a circuit breaker after the benchmark KOSPI index plunged more than 8%, forcing a temporary trading halt. The dramatic selloff comes as investors retreat from AI and tech stocks amid global market jitters.

headlines.headline: UN says children dying in Cuba due to strict US sanctions
headlines.summary: Tighter US sanctions on Cuba have led to a spike in infant mortality and plummeting survival rates for child cancer patients, according to a UN report highlighting humanitarian consequences of the economic measures.

headlines.headline: Trump administration seeks to revoke US citizenship of 17 immigrants
headlines.summary: The push to denaturalize more immigrants is the latest sign that the administration is setting its sights on the legal immigration system, expanding efforts beyond undocumented immigrants.

headlines.headline: Houthis Ban Israeli Shipping From Red Sea Amid Iran-Israel Conflict
headlines.summary: Yemen's Iran-aligned Houthis announced a ban on Israeli maritime navigation in the Red Sea, escalating threats to global shipping routes as the broader Iran-Israel conflict intensifies.

perspectives.angle: Houthis threaten ban on Israeli ships in Red Sea amid Iran conflict
perspectives.summary: Yemens Iran-aligned Houthis announced they would ban Israeli maritime navigation in the Red Sea, escalating threats to global shipping routes.

headlines.headline: EU Sanctions IRGC Navy Unit Over Strait of Hormuz Blockade
headlines.summary: The European Union imposed sanctions on two Iranian individuals and an IRGC naval unit for restricting maritime traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, implementing asset freezes and travel bans.

headlines.headline: French Fighter Jet Shoots Down Drone in Latvian Airspace During NATO Mission
headlines.summary: A French Rafale fighter on NATO air patrol shot down a drone that entered Latvian airspace, marking what appears to be the first such engagement in Europe and underscoring growing drone threats near Russian borders.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Foils Russian Plot to Assassinate Senior Military Intelligence Official
headlines.summary: Ukrainian authorities announced they had thwarted a Russian plan to assassinate a senior military intelligence official, according to police statements amid ongoing tensions between the two countries.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine Foils Russian Plot to Assassinate Military Intelligence Official
perspectives.summary: Ukraine's National Police detained a suspect allegedly planning to murder Andrii Yusov, spokesperson for Ukraine's military intelligence, in a thwarted assassination plot.

headlines.headline: Trump Says Israel and Iran Seeking Immediate Ceasefire
headlines.summary: US President Donald Trump stated both Israel and Iran are looking to do an immediate ceasefire, and that final negotiations on peace are proceeding. The announcement came as both sides halted strikes.

perspectives.angle: Netanyahu Defies Trump on Iran, Exposing US-Israel Policy Rift
perspectives.summary: President Trump has voiced frustration with Netanyahu over Israel's military actions, but analysts question whether Washington can effectively restrain Israeli operations in the region.

headlines.headline: Republican Senators Warn Key US Surveillance Program May Lapse
headlines.summary: Senators warned that a critical US surveillance authority could expire this week after bipartisan opposition to President Trump's intelligence pick. The program under debate allows warrantless collection of foreign communications.

headlines.headline: Iran to Charge Transit Fees for Ships Passing Through Strait of Hormuz
headlines.summary: Iran's ambassador to Moscow announced the strategic waterway will remain open but under new conditions set by Iran and Oman, including transit fees—a move that could reshape global energy shipping.

perspectives.angle: Iran says Hormuz Strait will reopen with transit fees
perspectives.summary: Iran's ambassador to Moscow said the strategic Strait of Hormuz will be open but under new conditions set by Iran and Oman, including transit fees, as the conflict with the US continues.

headlines.headline: French jet on NATO mission shoots down drone in Latvian airspace
headlines.summary: A French Rafale fighter jet shot down a drone that entered NATO member Latvia's airspace from Russia, the latest in a series of security incidents along Europe's eastern border.

perspectives.angle: NATO Fighter Shoots Down Drone Over Latvia in First Such Incident
perspectives.summary: A French Rafale on NATO patrol downed a drone that entered Latvian airspace from Russia, marking the first time NATO aircraft shot down an aerial threat over the Baltic state.

headlines.headline: Russia warns Armenia of new trade curbs after pro-Western election
headlines.summary: Russia signaled further restrictions on Armenian imports days after PM Pashinyan's election victory reinforced the country's pivot away from Moscow toward the West.

headlines.headline: US imposes visa restrictions on Nicaraguans over indigenous leader death
headlines.summary: The United States imposed additional visa restrictions on more than 100 Nicaraguan officials and their families over the death of indigenous leader Brooklyn Rivera.

headlines.headline: French NATO jet shoots down drone in Latvian airspace
headlines.summary: A French Rafale on NATO duty downed a drone that entered from Russia, the latest security incident along Europe's eastern border as Ukraine war intensifies.

perspectives.angle: NATO Jets Shoot Down Drone Over Latvia in First Such Incident
perspectives.summary: A French Rafale fighter shot down a drone that entered Latvian airspace from Russia, marking the first time NATO jets have downed an aircraft over Baltic territory amid spillover from the Ukraine war.

headlines.headline: China exports set for strong May on front-loaded orders and chip demand
headlines.summary: Export growth likely strengthened as firms rushed orders before Gulf war energy impacts, while global semiconductor and AI component demand remained robust.

headlines.headline: Pakistan-Administered Kashmir Clashes Kill 11 Ahead of Protest Rally
headlines.summary: At least 11 people were killed and more than 70 injured in clashes between protesters and security forces in Pakistan-administered Kashmir ahead of a Tuesday rally called by the banned Joint Awami Action Committee.

perspectives.angle: Pakistan-Administered Kashmir Court Clears Path for Refugee Elections
perspectives.summary: The top court in Pakistan-administered Kashmir upheld 12 legislative seats reserved for Kashmiri refugees, clearing the way for upcoming elections in the disputed territory.

headlines.headline: Amazon Signs Multi-Billion Dollar Deal With Corning for US Fiber Optics Production
headlines.summary: Amazon announced a multi-billion dollar agreement with specialty glass maker Corning to boost US production of optical fiber and connectivity products used in data centers, creating 1,000 jobs in North Carolina.

headlines.headline: EU Sanctions IRGC Officials Over Hormuz Blockade Participation
headlines.summary: The European Union imposed sanctions on two Iranian officials and an IRGC unit for their involvement in the Strait of Hormuz blockade, marking the first use of new sanctions powers.

headlines.headline: Turkish Forces Harass Aircraft Carrying European Defense Ministers to Cyprus
headlines.summary: Cyprus lodged formal complaints after Turkish forces interfered with aircraft carrying Greek, French, and other European defense ministers to the island nation.

headlines.headline: IMF Chief Warns World Unprepared for Mounting Crises
headlines.summary: IMF Managing Director Kristalina Georgieva warned that after years of consecutive crises, the global economy remains vulnerable to accumulating shocks, from geopolitical conflicts to economic instability.

headlines.headline: European Leaders Back Ukraine-Russia Ceasefire Talks
headlines.summary: Britain, Germany and France expressed support for direct talks between Zelenskiy and Putin to secure a ceasefire, marking a significant diplomatic push as European leaders coordinate their stance on ending the conflict.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine and Russia Trade Air Attacks as Zelenskyy Pushes for Direct Talks
perspectives.summary: Russia and Ukraine exchanged deadly air attacks hours after President Zelenskyy met with European leaders to push for direct negotiations. The Ukrainian leader expressed willingness to freeze current battle lines to end the war.

perspectives.angle: Zelensky's European allies set five conditions for peace talks with Russia
perspectives.summary: Britain, Germany and France outlined five conditions for Ukraine-Russia peace negotiations following talks with President Zelensky. The conditions come as Zelensky signaled willingness to freeze current battle lines to achieve peace.

perspectives.angle: European Leaders Support Ukraine Ceasefire Talks, Bolster Missile Defenses
perspectives.summary: British PM Starmer hosted Zelensky, Macron, and Merz to discuss ceasefire negotiations with Russia. The leaders emphasized urgent need to strengthen Ukraine's ballistic missile defenses.

headlines.headline: Japan Economy Cools on Weak Capital Expenditure
headlines.summary: Japan lost economic momentum in Q1 as revised GDP data showed sluggish capital expenditure, pointing to challenges ahead due to Middle East conflict and global uncertainty.

headlines.headline: Trump walks out of NBC interview on Iran strategy
headlines.summary: Former President Trump stormed out of a tense NBC interview, calling it "fake dirty press" when reporters pushed back on his Iran war strategy positions.

headlines.headline: French Army has recruits but lacks equipment
headlines.summary: Frances deputy army chief reports a recruitment surplus but critical equipment shortages, highlighting the gap between personnel growth and military hardware capacity.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo XIV and Spain's Sánchez unite against Trump policies
headlines.summary: Pope Leo XIV met with Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez in Rome on Monday, discussing shared opposition to President Trump's policies. The meeting highlights growing international coordination against US trade and diplomatic moves.

headlines.headline: Amnesty: Cambodia scam centers still operating despite crackdowns
headlines.summary: Dozens of suspected global scam compounds in Cambodia remain operational despite months-long crackdown by authorities, Amnesty International reported.

perspectives.angle: Amnesty: Cambodia scam centre crackdown ineffective
perspectives.summary: A new Amnesty report finds that Cambodias performative crackdowns have failed to stop scam centres exploiting workers, despite government claims of progress.

headlines.headline: Meta sues Israeli spyware firm NSO Group for violating WhatsApp injunction
headlines.summary: Meta filed a federal court contempt order against NSO Group for violating a permanent injunction that barred the firm from targeting WhatsApp and its users.

headlines.headline: UK Prime Minister Starmer set to ban harmful social media for under-16s
headlines.summary: British PM Keir Starmer is expected to announce a ban on harmful online platforms for children under 16 while maintaining access to safer forms of social media.

headlines.headline: UN urges Afghan Taliban to end arrests of women over dress rules
headlines.summary: The United Nations mission in Afghanistan expressed concern over arrests of women for failing to comply with dress requirements and urged Taliban authorities to treat all people equally.

headlines.headline: Japanese Yen Lingers Above ¥160 to Dollar as BOJ Meeting Approaches
headlines.summary: The yen traded back above ¥160 to the dollar, testing levels not seen in decades as markets await the Bank of Japan policy meeting. The currency remains under pressure from widening US-Japan interest rate differentials.

headlines.headline: Armenian PM Pashinyan claims victory in parliamentary election
headlines.summary: Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan claimed victory in Armenia's parliamentary election, with early results showing his pro-Western party leading. The election was seen as a choice between closer ties with Europe or Russia.

perspectives.angle: Pashinyan claims victory in Armenian election amid violations allegations
perspectives.summary: Armenian PM Nikol Pashinyan claimed victory in parliamentary elections with turnout at 58.97%, though observers noted electoral violations and the loss of his two-thirds majority.

headlines.headline: France Expands Nuclear Deterrence Pact to Scandinavia as Norway Opens Talks
headlines.summary: Norway has become the latest ally to discuss joining France's extended nuclear deterrence initiative, as European defense cooperation deepens amid growing security concerns.

headlines.headline: India Issues Fresh Travel Advisory for Iran, Urges Nationals to Leave Immediately
headlines.summary: India has reiterated its travel advisory for Iran amid escalating regional tensions, advising Indian nationals to avoid travel and urging those present to depart immediately.

headlines.headline: EU Defence Ministers Convene in Nicosia With Ukraine War Set to Dominate Agenda
headlines.summary: The EU's 27 defence ministers are gathering in Cyprus on Monday, with the ongoing Ukraine conflict expected to be the primary focus of discussions.

headlines.headline: Gold Prices Decline as Middle East Reescalation Dampens Safe-Haven Demand
headlines.summary: Gold prices began the week with losses despite renewed Middle East tensions, as market dynamics shift amid the ongoing Israel-Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Israel Hits Iranian Targets Despite Trump Warning to Netanyahu
headlines.summary: Israel struck military targets in western and central Iran despite U.S. President Donald Trump reportedly telling Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu to refrain from further attacks. Trump stated Netanyahu would have to accept any US-Iran deal.

headlines.headline: Tsunami Alerts Across Asia After Philippines Quake, Evacuations Underway
headlines.summary: Tsunami warnings were issued for Indonesia, the Philippines, and Japan after a powerful earthquake off Mindanao. Officials warned of possible waves over one meter as coastal communities evacuated to higher ground.

headlines.headline: IRGC Claims Israel Used Air-Launched Ballistic Missiles in Iran Attack
headlines.summary: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps stated that Israel employed air-launched ballistic missiles to strike targets inside Iran, marking a significant escalation in military capabilities used in the conflict.

headlines.headline: Indonesian Political Heavyweights Position as Prabowo Falters
headlines.summary: Indonesia's next presidential election is not until 2029, but political heavyweights are already maneuvering for position amid signs of weakness in the current administration.

headlines.headline: Pashinyan Declares Victory in Armenia Election, Cementing Westward Shift
headlines.summary: Armenian Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan declared victory in parliamentary elections, with early results showing a commanding lead. The outcome would cement Armenia's pivot away from Russian influence toward Europe.

perspectives.angle: Pashinyan claims victory in Armenian election seen as test of Russia influence
perspectives.summary: Armenia's pro-Western PM won the parliamentary election despite what observers called blatant Russian interference, paving the way for faster pivot away from Moscow.

headlines.headline: OPEC+ Approves Fourth Oil Output Hike Since Hormuz Closure
headlines.summary: OPEC+ agreed to raise oil production targets by 188,000 barrels per day for July, the fourth increase since the Strait of Hormuz closed due to the US-Iran conflict. The move is largely symbolic given continued supply constraints.

perspectives.angle: OPEC+ Approves Fourth Oil Output Hike Since Hormuz Closure
perspectives.summary: OPEC+ approved another modest increase in oil output targets for July, marking the fourth hike since the Strait of Hormuz was disrupted. The move comes despite ongoing supply concerns from the Iran-Israel conflict.

headlines.headline: Japan Ground Self-Defense Force Holds Live-Fire Exercises With 3,000 Troops
headlines.summary: Japan's Ground Self-Defense Force conducted annual live-fire exercises near Mount Fuji with 3,000 troops participating, showcasing military readiness amid regional tensions.

headlines.headline: Japan Economy Loses Momentum in Q1 as Capital Expenditure Slumps
headlines.summary: Japan's economy lost momentum in the January-March quarter as sluggish capital expenditure pointed to challenges ahead from the Middle East conflict. Revised GDP data showed annualized growth of 1.8%, below initial estimates, as the Iran war impact weighs on the export-dependent economy.

headlines.headline: Iran threatens US targets after Lebanon escalation
headlines.summary: Iran's top negotiator warned that US bases and Israeli assets in the Middle East are legitimate targets following a US naval blockade of Iran and Israel's escalation in Lebanon.

headlines.headline: Peru presidential runoff too close to call
headlines.summary: Exit polls show Peru's presidential election runoff in a statistical tie as voters choose between competing visions for the country's future amid political turmoil.

perspectives.angle: Peru Votes in Tight Presidential Runoff Testing Latin America's Rightward Shift
perspectives.summary: Peruvians cast ballots in a close presidential runoff between pro-US conservative Keiko Fujimori and leftist Roberto Sánchez, a race that could determine whether Latin America's rightward trend continues or reverses.

headlines.headline: US troops and families adjust to new normal of Iran war
headlines.summary: The prolonged state of readiness in the US-Iran conflict is causing significant stress for service members and their families as the war reaches its 100th day.

perspectives.angle: US troops and families adjust to prolonged state of conflict with Iran
perspectives.summary: American military personnel and their families are adapting to an unusual state of conflict with Iran that is neither full-scale war nor peace, with ongoing tensions causing stress for service members stationed in the region.

headlines.headline: Iran World Cup team arrives in Mexico amid US tensions
headlines.summary: Iran's World Cup squad arrived in Tijuana for the tournament as US-Iran tensions remain high. Several support staff were denied US visas while players received theirs.

perspectives.angle: Iran national soccer team arrives in Mexico for World Cup training
perspectives.summary: Iran squad lands in Mexico ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup, where they will prepare before group matches in the United States. The team faces US, England, and Ukraine in the group stage.

headlines.headline: Iran Fires Missiles at Israel, Violating Ceasefire Agreement
headlines.summary: Iran launched ballistic missiles at Israel for the first time since the April ceasefire, marking a major escalation. Israel's military confirmed the attack, while Iran's state broadcaster acknowledged the strikes.

perspectives.angle: Trump Urges Restraint After Iran Fires Missiles at Israel
perspectives.summary: President Donald Trump urged restraint after Iran launched missiles at Israel in the first such attack since the ceasefire began. The escalation follows Israeli strikes on Beirut that killed at least two people, with the US pushing to preserve the fragile peace talks.

headlines.headline: Iran Suspends Flights at Tehran Airport Amid Escalation
headlines.summary: Iran civil aviation authority suspended all flights at Tehran International Airport until further notice, as regional tensions spike following Iran missile launches against Israel.

headlines.headline: China Expands Nuclear Arsenal to 620 Warheads: SIPRI
headlines.summary: Swedish think-tank SIPRI reports China has increased its nuclear warheads to 620 and may be increasing deployment as the New Start treaty expiry ends arms control efforts.

headlines.headline: Armenia Votes: Pashinyan Seeks Mandate for Westward Shift
headlines.summary: Armenians head to polls in parliamentary elections that could determine whether PM Pashinyan gets a mandate strong enough to reorient the country toward Europe amid tensions with Russia.

headlines.headline: UK Foreign Minister Calls for De-escalation Between Iran and Israel
headlines.summary: UK Foreign Minister Yvette Cooper urged both Iran and Israel to show restraint and de-escalate after Iran renewed missile attacks on Israel.

headlines.headline: Xi Jinping Visits North Korea as China Seeks to Counter US Influence
headlines.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping arrived in Pyongyang for a state visit, declaring China's unwavering policy to upgrade ties with North Korea. The summit signals Beijing's strategic focus on regional security amid tensions with Washington.

headlines.headline: France Warns of New Sanctions on Israeli Settlers Within Days
headlines.summary: France's foreign minister announced that additional sanctions on Israeli settlers could be imposed in coming days, protesting the escalation of illegal settlements in the West Bank and rising settler violence against Palestinians.

perspectives.angle: France threatens more sanctions on Israeli settlers over West Bank violence
perspectives.summary: French Foreign Minister Jean-Noel Barrot warns that additional sanctions could be imposed on Israeli settlers in coming days in response to illegal settlement expansion and violence against Palestinians.

headlines.headline: Armenia's Pro-Western Party Leads Election as Pashinyan Defies Russia
headlines.summary: Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan's Civil Contract party is leading with 54% in Armenia's parliamentary election, a shift away from Russian influence toward the West.

perspectives.angle: Armenias pro-Europe party wins election, cementing shift away from Russia
perspectives.summary: Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyans Civil Contract party won parliamentary elections, confirming Armenias pivot toward Europe and away from its traditional ally Russia despite Moscows pressure campaign.

headlines.headline: Kosovo's Kurti Wins Snap Election But Lacks Majority to End Crisis
headlines.summary: Prime Minister Albin Kurti's party won the most votes in Kosovo's third election in a year, but fell short of a majority amid a protracted political crisis.

perspectives.angle: Kosovo PM Kurti Wins Snap Election But Lacks Majority
perspectives.summary: Albin Kurtis Vetevendosje party won nearly 44% of the vote in Kosovo parliamentary election, but fell short of a majority needed to end the ongoing political crisis.

headlines.headline: Iran Rejects US Plan to Use Frozen Assets for Gulf Allies' Damages
headlines.summary: Tehran dismissed Washington's plan to use Iranian assets to compensate Gulf states for war damages, calling it unacceptable and warning regional governments.

perspectives.angle: US considers redirecting frozen Iranian assets to Gulf allies for reconstruction
perspectives.summary: The Trump administration is exploring using frozen Iranian assets to fund rebuilding efforts in Gulf states affected by the conflict, as ceasefire negotiations remain stalled over Tehran demand for access to $24 billion in oil revenue.

perspectives.angle: Iran Launches Ballistic Missiles Toward Gulf Allies as US Intercepts
perspectives.summary: Iran fired ballistic missiles toward Kuwait and Bahrain, with US forces intercepting most of them. The attack marks another escalation in the ongoing US-Iran conflict despite ceasefire talks.

headlines.headline: Peru Heads to Runoff as Sánchez Faces Fujimori in Polarized Race
headlines.summary: Peruvians vote in a presidential runoff between Roberto Sánchez and Keiko Fujimori, with EU observers deployed as the country faces deep political divisions.

headlines.headline: Canada's Carney Heads to Ireland, France Before G7 Summit
headlines.summary: Prime Minister Mark Carney will deepen ties with European partners before attending the G7 summit in Evian-les-Bains, with focus on reducing global tensions.

headlines.headline: Nepal FM Signals Diplomatic Approach to India Border Row
headlines.summary: Nepal Foreign Minister Shisir Khanal said no boundary is too complex if tackled with an open heart, signaling a diplomatic push to resolve territorial disputes with India and strengthen bilateral ties.

headlines.headline: US considers buying Chagos Islands from Mauritius
headlines.summary: The White House is weighing a plan to purchase the strategically important Chagos Islands from Mauritius, the Telegraph reports.

headlines.headline: Central African Republic agrees to accept third-country deportees from US
headlines.summary: The Trump administration strikes a deal with Central African Republic to accept migrants from other countries deported by the US, the latest in a series of opaque agreements with African states.

headlines.headline: The Atlantic: Ukraine Not Losing, Russia Not Winning as Momentum Shifts
headlines.summary: Gulf states and others now understand that the Russian narrative is wrong. Ukrainians continue to resist as the war enters a new phase with drone warfare reshaping the front lines.

headlines.headline: Taiwan Asserts Maritime Rights as Regional Tensions Escalate
headlines.summary: Taiwan emphasized that its rights over territorial waters and exclusive economic zone must not be violated by any country, amid heightened regional tensions in the Taiwan Strait.

headlines.headline: CBS News: Iran Accuses US of Ceasefire Violation as Stalemate Continues
headlines.summary: US military reports shooting down four Iranian drones headed toward Strait of Hormuz overnight. Peace talks remain deadlocked as both sides exchange accusations.

headlines.headline: Female Navy Officers Fear Career Cap After Hegseth Cuts Women From Promotions
headlines.summary: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth removed all women from a Navy promotion list, raising concerns about gender discrimination in the military and impacting career trajectories for female officers.

headlines.headline: UK Military Tearing Its Hair Out as Labour Dithering Endangers National Security
headlines.summary: Critics condemn Labour government failure to strengthen British Armed Forces while war rages in Europe, with calls for urgent defence investment amid growing Russian threat.

headlines.headline: South Africa's Ramaphosa vows crackdown on xenophobic violence amid migration tensions
headlines.summary: President Ramaphosa announced tougher immigration enforcement and penalties for employers hiring undocumented workers, while distancing the government from vigilante immigration checks.

perspectives.angle: Malawi Begins Evacuation of Nationals From South Africa Amid Xenophobia Crisis
perspectives.summary: The Government of Malawi announced a voluntary repatriation exercise for its nationals displaced in South Africa, with the first 150 citizens returning home as xenophobic violence spreads.

headlines.headline: Armenians vote in pivotal election with Russia-West tensions in focus
headlines.summary: Prime Minister Pashinyan's pro-Western ruling party faces opposition candidates favoring closer ties with Russia, in a vote that could reshape the South Caucasus country's geopolitical alignment.

perspectives.angle: Armenians Vote in Pivotal Election Testing Westward Shift Amid Russian Pressure
perspectives.summary: Armenia holds parliamentary elections seen as a test of PM Pashinyan's pivot away from Russia, with six pro-Russian opposition candidates arrested the day before the vote.

perspectives.angle: Armenia Holds Election That Could Reshape Ties With Russia and the West
perspectives.summary: Armenia votes Sunday in parliamentary elections that could pivot the country away from Russia toward closer Western ties, with Prime Minister Pashinyan seeking a mandate for constitutional reforms.

headlines.headline: Iran's negotiator threatens US bases and Israeli assets over Lebanon escalation
headlines.summary: Iran's top negotiator declared US bases and Israeli assets in the Middle East "legitimate targets" following Israel's strikes on Beirut and the US naval blockade of Iran.

headlines.headline: Taiwan Coast Guard Expels Chinese Ships from Restricted Waters
headlines.summary: Taiwan's coast guard expelled four Chinese government vessels that entered restricted waters off the island's south, with both sides exchanging warnings in a rare escalation of tensions across the Taiwan Strait.

perspectives.angle: Taiwan Reports China Coast Guard Harassing Commercial Shipping
perspectives.summary: Taiwans coast guard accused Chinas coast guard of harassing commercial shipping off its coast, telling merchant vessels to ignore Chinese inquiries in a new escalation of maritime tensions.

headlines.headline: IDF reveals major Hezbollah command tunnel network in southern Lebanon
headlines.summary: The Israeli military published footage of a Hezbollah tunnel system near Beaufort Castle, showing sophisticated infrastructure built with Iranian support for coordinating attacks.

headlines.headline: US sending third-country deportees to Central African Republic
headlines.summary: The Trump administration has arranged for the impoverished Central African Republic to accept deportees from third countries amid widespread protests against US immigration policies.

headlines.headline: China activates flood emergency response in six regions amid heavy rain forecast
headlines.summary: Chinese authorities warned of rising river levels, flash floods and mountain torrents as rainstorms are expected to continue through Tuesday across six provinces.

headlines.headline: Iran World Cup Team Lands in Mexico Amid US Visa Dispute
headlines.summary: Irans soccer team arrived in Tijuana, Mexico for the World Cup as Tehran accused Washington of obstructing team preparations. The diplomatic row highlights tensions between the two nations even as they negotiate an end to the ongoing conflict.

headlines.headline: Beijing sends largest patrol ship east of Taiwan after Japan-Philippine boundary talks
headlines.summary: China deployed its largest patrol vessel near Taiwan following Japan-Philippine maritime boundary discussions. Communist Party mouthpiece warned the two countries are becoming a "source of trouble" and putting regional peace at risk.

perspectives.angle: Japan-Philippines maritime boundary talks exclude Taiwan from negotiations
perspectives.summary: Japan and the Philippines announced plans to delimit maritime boundaries in waters east of Taiwan, drawing protests from both Taipei and Beijing. The dispute reveals Taiwan's growing vulnerability as its interests are decided in negotiations where it has no representation.

headlines.headline: Finland's Stubb urges Europe to step up and lead peace talks with Russia
headlines.summary: Finnish President Alexander Stubb called for Europe to take a more active role in leading peace negotiations with Russia, as the continent grapples with how to resolve the ongoing conflict in Ukraine.

headlines.headline: UK Royal Navy's entire submarine fleet out of service for maintenance
headlines.summary: Britain's entire fleet of Astute-class submarines are currently out of service waiting for maintenance, raising concerns about the UK's ability to deter Russian stealth submarines and protect its Vanguard fleet.

headlines.headline: Cabinet bodyguards working without security clearance
headlines.summary: Bodyguards protecting senior UK Cabinet ministers have been operating without proper security clearance, according to a leaked letter raising serious concerns about ministerial safety.

headlines.headline: Global airlines slash 2026 profit forecast on fuel shock from Iran war
headlines.summary: The International Air Transport Association (IATA) downgraded its 2026 profit outlook for the global airline industry, citing fuel price volatility and geopolitical disruption from the Iran conflict. Profits are expected to halve despite resilient passenger demand.

perspectives.angle: Global Airlines Slash 2026 Profit Forecast on Iran War Fuel Shock
perspectives.summary: The global airline industry nearly halved its 2026 profit forecast to $23 billion, citing fuel price volatility from the Middle East conflict. Middle East carriers are expected to post a $4.3 billion loss as passenger demand remains resilient.

headlines.headline: Trump rules out unfreezing Iranian assets before peace deal
headlines.summary: In an NBC News interview, US President Donald Trump stated he would not unfreeze Iranian assets or lift sanctions until a comprehensive peace agreement is reached. The stance could complicate ongoing ceasefire negotiations.

perspectives.angle: Iran demands $24 billion in frozen assets as condition for US peace deal progress
perspectives.summary: Iranian military adviser Mohsen Rezaei said negotiations with the US have reached a deadlock, tying progress to the release of $24 billion in frozen Iranian assets. Tensions remain high following US strikes on Iranian coastal sites.

headlines.headline: One killed, five wounded in central Israel terror shooting spree
headlines.summary: A terrorist attack in communities bordering the West Bank north of Tel Aviv left one person dead and five wounded, with two in serious condition. One perpetrator was shot during the rampage.

perspectives.angle: One Dead, Four Wounded in Suspected Terror Shooting in Central Israel
perspectives.summary: A shooting attack in Kochav Ya'ir area left one person dead and at least four wounded; Israeli security forces captured one suspect and are searching for another.

headlines.headline: Trump-Xi summit on Taiwan offered more than optics
headlines.summary: The meeting between Chinese and US presidents laid the groundwork for more predictable China-US relations, including on the Taiwan issue, beyond just symbolic photo opportunities.

headlines.headline: Pakistan's Interior Minister in Tehran to Mediate Iran-US Talks
headlines.summary: Pakistan's Mohsin Naqvi visited Tehran carrying a special letter as regional powers push to revive Iran-US negotiations, while US forces downed additional Iranian drones over the Strait of Hormuz.

perspectives.angle: Pakistani Interior Minister Arrives in Tehran for Iran-US Mediation Efforts
perspectives.summary: Pakistan's interior minister traveled to Tehran amid ongoing diplomatic efforts to mediate between Iran and the United States as tensions continue to escalate in the Gulf region.

headlines.headline: Russia downs 339 Ukrainian drones in massive 13-hour interception barrage
headlines.summary: Russian air defenses intercepted and destroyed 339 Ukrainian drones over a 13-hour period across multiple regions including Moscow, in one of the largest drone attacks of the war.

perspectives.angle: Russia says it intercepted 339 Ukrainian drones over 13 hours
perspectives.summary: Russia's Defence Ministry reported intercepting and destroying 339 Ukrainian drones over a 13-hour period across multiple Russian regions, including Moscow, in one of the largest drone barrages of the war.

headlines.headline: Russia signals openness to new US relationship as Ukraine war stalls
headlines.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin has expressed openness to a new relationship with the United States, even as peace talks show no progress in ending the Ukraine conflict. The overture comes amid continued battlefield deadlock.

perspectives.angle: Russia signals openness to new relationship with US amid Ukraine deadlock
perspectives.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin has sought to project a bullish view of Russia's position even as talks appear no closer to ending the war. The overture comes as Ukraine continues drone strikes deep into Russian territory.

headlines.headline: Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant restores power supply after outage
headlines.summary: The Russian-controlled Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant in Ukraine restored its power supply Saturday after an outage, according to plant management. The facility has faced repeated power disruptions during the conflict.

perspectives.angle: Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant restores power supply after emergency
perspectives.summary: The Russian-installed management of Ukraine's Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant said it restored the Ferrosplavnaya-1 power line that supplies electricity to the facility, amid ongoing nuclear safety concerns.

headlines.headline: Nigerian military rescues 360 hostages from Borno mountain hideout
headlines.summary: Security forces freed 360 men, women, and children held by kidnappers in a mountain enclave in Borno state, in a major operation against militant groups.

perspectives.angle: Nigerian Army Rescues 360 Hostages from Boko Haram Stronghold
perspectives.summary: Nigerian forces freed 360 kidnapped victims from a Boko Haram enclave in the Mandara Mountains after weeks of intelligence-gathering and surveillance operations.

headlines.headline: Greek police arrest Palestinian man on Crete over Hamas-linked terror plot
headlines.summary: A 37-year-old Palestinian was detained on suspicion of planning terrorist attacks and receiving training abroad, linked to four similar arrests in Cyprus.

perspectives.angle: Arrested Palestinian in Crete Confesses Hamas Membership, Training in Indonesia
perspectives.summary: Greek counter-terrorism officers obtained a full confession from the 37-year-old Palestinian arrested on Crete, who admitted to Hamas membership and training in Indonesia.

headlines.headline: Armenia arrests six opposition candidates day before election
headlines.summary: Armenian authorities detained six candidates from a pro-Russian opposition party on Saturday, one day before parliamentary elections seen as a test of the government's pivot away from Moscow toward the West.

headlines.headline: Pakistan delivers special letter to Iran's supreme leader
headlines.summary: Pakistan's Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi traveled to Tehran with messages from the prime minister and army chief as diplomatic efforts intensify to end the US-Iran conflict and prevent further escalation in the Gulf.

headlines.headline: Greek police arrest Palestinian man suspected of Hamas attack plot
headlines.summary: Police on the Greek island of Crete arrested a 37-year-old Palestinian man on suspicion of planning terrorist attacks and belonging to the militant group Hamas, authorities said.

headlines.headline: European Central Bank Begins Interest Rate Hikes
headlines.summary: The ECB starts raising interest rates, marking a significant shift in European monetary policy as the continent grapples with economic pressures from ongoing geopolitical tensions.

headlines.headline: Singapore Blocks Anti-Indian Content, Traces Origin to China
headlines.summary: Singapore authorities invoke the Online Criminal Harms Act to restrict access to posts targeting the Indian community, saying the content likely originated from China-based sources.

headlines.headline: Trump Faces Growing Republican Resistance in Congress
headlines.summary: Republican lawmakers are increasingly breaking with Trump on key policy issues including the Iran conflict as midterm election pressures mount, potentially threatening his agenda.

perspectives.angle: Trump Faces Growing Republican Resistance as Midterm Pressures Mount
